examples.sentence: 明日は試験があります。そこで今晩勉強しようと思います。
examples.translation: There is an exam tomorrow. Therefore, I plan to study tonight.
examples.translationEn: There is an exam tomorrow. Therefore, I plan to study tonight.
examples.romaji: Ashita wa shiken ga arimasu. Soko de konban benkyou shiyou to omoimasu.

examples.sentence: このレストランは混んでいます。そこで別の場所でランチを食べましょう。
examples.translation: This restaurant is crowded. Therefore, let's eat lunch at a different place.
examples.translationEn: This restaurant is crowded. Therefore, let's eat lunch at a different place.
examples.romaji: Kono resutoran wa kondeimasu. Soko de betsu no basho de ranchi wo tabemashou.

examples.sentence: 彼女はアレルギーがある。そこで彼女のために別の料理を作ります。
examples.translation: She has an allergy. Therefore, I'll cook a different dish for her.
examples.translationEn: She has an allergy. Therefore, I'll cook a different dish for her.
examples.romaji: Kanojo wa arerugii ga aru. Soko de kanojo no tame ni betsu no ryouri wo tsukurimasu.

examples.sentence: 傘を持っていない。そこで雨が止むまで待ちます。
examples.translation: I don't have an umbrella. Therefore, I'll wait until the rain stops.
examples.translationEn: I don't have an umbrella. Therefore, I'll wait until the rain stops.
examples.romaji: Kasa wo motte inai. Soko de ame ga yamu made machimasu.

examples.sentence: 彼は勉強ができる。それが天才というものだ。
examples.translation: He can study well. That's what you call a genius.
examples.translationEn: He can study well. That's what you call a genius.
examples.romaji: Kare wa benkyou ga dekiru. Sore ga tensai to iu mono da.

examples.sentence: 美味しい料理を作る。それが私の趣味なんです。
examples.translation: I make delicious food. That's my hobby.
examples.translationEn: I make delicious food. That's my hobby.
examples.romaji: Oishii ryouri wo tsukuru. Sore ga watashi no shumi nan desu.

examples.sentence: 彼女は私を助けてくれる。それが友達というものだ。
examples.translation: She helps me. That's what a friend is.
examples.translationEn: She helps me. That's what a friend is.
examples.romaji: Kanojo wa watashi wo tasukete kureru. Sore ga tomodachi to iu mono da.

examples.sentence: 一人で長い旅をする。それが自由というものだ。
examples.translation: Traveling alone for a long time. That's what freedom is.
examples.translationEn: Traveling alone for a long time. That's what freedom is.
examples.romaji: Hitori de nagai tabi wo suru. Sore ga jiyuu to iu mono da.

examples.sentence: 昨日は雨が降っていた。それで公園に行かなかった。
examples.translation: It was raining yesterday. So, I didn't go to the park.
examples.translationEn: It was raining yesterday. So, I didn't go to the park.
examples.romaji: Kinou wa ame ga futte ita. Sore de kouen ni ikanakatta.

examples.sentence: 試験に落ちた。それで再試験を受けなければならない。
examples.translation: I failed the exam. So, I have to take a re-exam.
examples.translationEn: I failed the exam. So, I have to take a re-exam.
examples.romaji: Shiken ni ochita. Sore de sai shiken wo ukenakereba naranai.

examples.sentence: 部屋が寒かった。それで暖房をつけた。
examples.translation: The room was cold. So, I turned on the heater.
examples.translationEn: The room was cold. So, I turned on the heater.
examples.romaji: Heya ga samukatta. Sore de danbou wo tsuketa.

examples.sentence: 今日は休みだ。それで映画を見に行く。
examples.translation: Today is a holiday. So, I'm going to watch a movie.
examples.translationEn: Today is a holiday. So, I'm going to watch a movie.
examples.romaji: Kyou wa yasumi da. Sore de eiga wo mi ni iku.

examples.sentence: 今日は日曜日です。それでも、仕事に行かなければなりません。
examples.translation: Today is Sunday. Nevertheless, I have to go to work.
examples.translationEn: Today is Sunday. Nevertheless, I have to go to work.
examples.romaji: Kyou wa nichiyoubi desu. Sore demo, shigoto ni ikanakereba narimasen.

examples.sentence: 彼は若いです。それでも、経験が豊富です。
examples.translation: He is young. Even so, he has a lot of experience.
examples.translationEn: He is young. Even so, he has a lot of experience.
examples.romaji: Kare wa wakai desu. Sore demo, keiken ga houfu desu.

examples.sentence: 雨が降っています。それでも、散歩に行きたいです。
examples.translation: It's raining. Still, I want to go for a walk.
examples.translationEn: It's raining. Still, I want to go for a walk.
examples.romaji: Ame ga futteimasu. Sore demo, sanpo ni ikitai desu.

examples.sentence: 試験の結果が悪かったです。それでも、諦めません。
examples.translation: The exam results were bad. But I won't give up.
examples.translationEn: The exam results were bad. But I won't give up.
examples.romaji: Shiken no kekka ga warukatta desu. Sore demo, akiramenai.

examples.sentence: 彼は頭がいい。それなのに、試験に落ちた。
examples.translation: He is smart. Despite that, he failed the exam.
examples.translationEn: He is smart. Despite that, he failed the exam.
examples.romaji: Kare wa atama ga ii. Sore nanoni, shiken ni ochita.

examples.sentence: 私は早く起きた。それなのに、バスに遅れた。
examples.translation: I woke up early. Even so, I was late for the bus.
examples.translationEn: I woke up early. Even so, I was late for the bus.
examples.romaji: Watashi wa hayaku okita. Sore nanoni, basu ni okureta.

examples.sentence: 昨日は晴れていた。それなのに、彼は傘を持っていた。
examples.translation: It was sunny yesterday. Despite that, he had an umbrella.
examples.translationEn: It was sunny yesterday. Despite that, he had an umbrella.
examples.romaji: Kinou wa harete ita. Sore nanoni, kare wa kasa wo motte ita.

examples.sentence: 彼女は運動が苦手だ。それなのに、マラソンを走ることにした。
examples.translation: She is not good at sports. Nevertheless, she decided to run a marathon.
examples.translationEn: She is not good at sports. Nevertheless, she decided to run a marathon.
examples.romaji: Kanojo wa undou ga nigate da. Sore nanoni, marason wo hashiru koto ni shita.

examples.sentence: 試験が近い。それなら、もっと勉強しなければならない。
examples.translation: The exam is coming soon. If that's the case, I must study more.
examples.translationEn: The exam is coming soon. If that's the case, I must study more.
examples.romaji: Shiken ga chikai. Sore nara, motto benkyou shinakereba naranai.

examples.sentence: 明日は雨が降るだろう。それなら、傘を持って行こう。
examples.translation: It will probably rain tomorrow. In that case, let's bring an umbrella.
examples.translationEn: It will probably rain tomorrow. In that case, let's bring an umbrella.
examples.romaji: Ashita wa ame ga furudarou. Sore nara, kasa wo motte ikou.

examples.sentence: 彼らはもう帰った。それなら、私も帰ろう。
examples.translation: They have already left. If that's the case, I'll go home too.
examples.translationEn: They have already left. If that's the case, I'll go home too.
examples.romaji: Karera wa mou kaetta. Sore nara, watashi mo kaerou.

examples.sentence: あのレストランは予約がいっぱいだ。それなら、別の場所を探そう。
examples.translation: Reservations at that restaurant are full. In that case, let's find another place.
examples.translationEn: Reservations at that restaurant are full. In that case, let's find another place.
examples.romaji: Ano resutoran wa yoyaku ga ippai da. Sore nara, betsu no basho wo sagasou.

examples.grammarPoint: A それはそうと B。
examples.sentence: それはそうと、今日の天気はいいですね。
examples.translation: By the way, the weather is nice today, isn't it?
examples.translationEn: By the way, the weather is nice today, isn't it?
examples.romaji: Sore wa sou to, kyou no tenki wa ii desu ne.

examples.grammarPoint: A それはそうと B。
examples.sentence: 映画は面白かったです。それはそうと、この店のラーメンはおいしいですよ。
examples.translation: The movie was interesting. Speaking of which, this restaurant's ramen is delicious.
examples.translationEn: The movie was interesting. Speaking of which, this restaurant's ramen is delicious.
examples.romaji: Eiga wa omoshirokatta desu. Sore wa sou to, kono mise no raamen wa oishii desu yo.

examples.grammarPoint: A それはそうと B。
examples.sentence: 明日から夏休みですね。それはそうと、新しいカフェがオープンしたみたいです。
examples.translation: Summer vacation starts tomorrow, huh? By the way, it seems a new cafe has opened.
examples.translationEn: Summer vacation starts tomorrow, huh? By the way, it seems a new cafe has opened.
examples.romaji: Ashita kara natsu yasumi desu ne. Sore wa sou to, atarashii kafe ga oopun shita mitai desu.

examples.grammarPoint: A それはそうと B。
examples.sentence: 昨日の試験は難しかった。それはそうと、週末にパーティーがあるのを知ってますか？
examples.translation: Yesterday's exam was difficult. Speaking of which, do you know there's a party this weekend?
examples.translationEn: Yesterday's exam was difficult. Speaking of which, do you know there's a party this weekend?
examples.romaji: Kinou no shiken wa muzukashikatta. Sore wa sou to, shuumatsu ni paatii ga aru no wo shitte masu ka?

examples.sentence: このレストランは値段が高い。だが、味はとてもいい。
examples.translation: This restaurant is expensive. But, the taste is very good.
examples.translationEn: This restaurant is expensive. But, the taste is very good.
examples.romaji: Kono resutoran wa nedan ga takai. Daga, aji wa totemo ii.

examples.sentence: 彼は疲れている。だが、仕事を終わらせるまで休まない。
examples.translation: He is tired. However, he will not rest until he finishes work.
examples.translationEn: He is tired. However, he will not rest until he finishes work.
examples.romaji: Kare wa tsukarete iru. Daga, shigoto o owaraseru made yasumanai.

examples.sentence: 昨日は寒かった。だが、今日は暖かい。
examples.translation: Yesterday was cold. But, today is warm.
examples.translationEn: Yesterday was cold. But, today is warm.
examples.romaji: Kinou wa samukatta. Daga, kyou wa atatakai.

examples.sentence: 彼女は外見は地味だ。だが、性格が素晴らしい。
examples.translation: Her appearance is plain. However, she has a wonderful personality.
examples.translationEn: Her appearance is plain. However, she has a wonderful personality.
examples.romaji: Kanojo wa gaiken wa jimi da. Daga, seikaku ga subarashii.
